rulings.Case Title: VLADIMIR MIROV VS. ARKADY BERGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Assignment Of Right To Payment Re Socom
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 28, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT ARKADY BERGER'S Motion For Assignment Of Right To Payment Re Socom. Denied. Judgment creditor Alex Berger has not established that judgment debtor Gary Varum currently receives rent payments for the 480 Arlington property. Rather, rent payments are received by Mr. Varum's son, who is not a judgment debtor in this action. The Court cannot make a determination in the context of this motion about whether the transfer of rights from Mr. Varum to his son was fraudulent. Rather, the Uniform Fraudulent Transfer Act requires a separate action to set aside a fraudulent transfer. (See Civ. Code sec. 3439.07(a).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order on the Judicial Council form with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SB)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN LENGYEL et al VS. NICHOLAS PREPOUSES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion Of Defendant Devendra Patel For Terminating Sanctions Against Plaintiff Lakeysha White
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 28, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT DEVENDRA PATEL'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against Plaintiff Lakeysha White. Pro Tem Judge Margaret Niver, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Devendra Patel's motion for terminating sanctions against Plaintiff Lakeysha White is DENIED. A terminating sanction is a drastic remedy and it is not clear from the evidence submitted by Defendant that lesser sanctions would not be sufficient to secure Plaintiff's compliance with the discovery rules. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to margaret.niver@pillsburylaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SAM TRINH VS. AMEN CLINIC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT By Defendant Amen Clinics, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 28, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT AMEN CLINICS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant meets its burden of production by demonstrating that Dr. Elwyn was an independent contractor. (Watkins Dec., pars. 2-3). In opposition, plaintiff creates a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant is liable based upon principles of ostensible authority. Dr. Elwyn uses defendant's logo and website. Defendant held Dr. Elwyn out as its agent/employee. See CC 2300; 2317; Yanchor v. Kagan (1971) 22 Cal.App.3d 544, 550. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SB)

rulings.Case Title: PETER CASTRO VS. OLD REPUBLIC TITLE COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Old Republic Title Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 28, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT OLD REPUBLIC TITLE COMPANY'S Motion To Compel Discovery, And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Margaret Niver, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Old Republic Title Company's motion to compel Plaintiff Peter Castro to produce documents and to appear for deposition, and for sanctions, is DENIED. It is undisputed that after Defendant filed its motion to compel, Plaintiff produced documents and his counsel provided 11 dates on which Plaintiff was available to appear for deposition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to margaret.niver@pillsburylaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: LESLIE SPECTOR VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Have Requests For Admissions Deemed Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $1,560.00 [Ccp Sections 2033.010 Et. Seq.]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 28, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF LESLIE SPECTOR'S Motion For Order To Have Requests For Admissions Deemed Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $1,560.00 [CCP Sections 2033.010 Et. Seq.] Pro Tem Judge Margaret Niver, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff Leslie Spector's motion to have the requests for admissions she served on defendant LV Grill LLC dba Artesano be deemed admitted and for monetary sanctions in the amount of $1,560 is DENIED. Defendant timely served verified responses to Plaintiff's first set of requests for admission, thus there is no basis under Code of Civil Procedure section 2033.280 for Plaintiff to seek an order that her requests for admission be deemed admitted by Defendant. Both parties' requests for sanctions are DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to margaret.niver@pillsburylaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ZHEN QIANG ZHANG VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 28, 2015, Line 7, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the tenth cause of action for wrongful termination in violation of public policy is sustained without leave to amend. In Miklosy v. Regents of University of California, 44 Cal.4th 876, the California Supreme Court held that a state common law claim for wrongful termination in violation of public policy, known as a Tameny action, cannot be brought against a public entity. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: NATHANIEL HOLMES VS. DIPASQUALE J PETER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 28, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT JOSEPH DIPASQUALE'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons. Denied. The only evidence submitted in support of Defendant's motion is a declaration from Defendant's counsel who vaguely asserts that Defendant does not work or reside at the address where the purported substitute service occurred. (Declaration of David Lane, par. 4.) Defendant's counsel does not state facts establishing that he has personal knowledge about where Defendant works, nor does he state any specific facts to support this general assertion, i.e., where Defendant actually does work. Plaintiff's evidence sufficiently rebuts this vague assertion and establishes that Defendant works at the address where the substitute service occurred. (Declaration of Bryan Harrison, Exs. 1-6.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SB)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER ADAMS VS. FLORIO ASUNCION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 28, 2015, Line 11, DEFENDANT YELLOW CAB COOPERATIVE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The First Amended Complaint ("FAC") does not comply with CRC 2.112 which requires that each cause of action must specifically state its number, its nature, the party asserting it, and the party against whom it is directed. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to attach causes of action to the form complaint, as required by Paragraph 10. Additionally, the FAC does not allege any facts establishing a basis for imposing liability against Defendant Yellow Cab Cooperative, Inc. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to allege facts establishing a basis for liability as to Defendant Yellow Cab. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number.
